meta.disclaimer: Reasoned analytical estimates from live team-news research, not live bookmaker prices. Fun-money only — always check the actual odds before you stake.
meta.method: Per-match deep research on both squads (probable XIs, form, injuries, set-piece + penalty takers) via live web search, refreshed daily and again near kickoff when lineups are confirmed.

win.reason: Opta supercomputer gives Portugal 54.5% win vs 23.2% DR Congo, 22.3% draw. Portugal unbeaten in last 5 (3 straight friendly wins pre-tournament), arguably best midfield in the tournament (Vitinha, B.Fernandes, J.Neves, Bernardo Silva) plus deep attack. DR Congo set up to defend deep in a compact 4-5-1/5-3-2 and counter, which caps Portugal's margin but not the result.

scorers.note: Captain at his sixth WC, top scorer in Portugal's qualifying group (5 goals), penalty taker, scored in WC openers before (Ghana 2022). Central striker in Martinez's 4-2-3-1.
Sharp PSG finisher and the natural No.9 alternative/sub; reliable late-game scorer if Ronaldo is rotated or Portugal chase a second-half goal vs a tiring deep block.
Back in his favoured No.10 role, on penalties/free-kicks as backup, arrives in the box; record 21 PL assists this season but also a goal threat from range.
Direct wide threat cutting in on his stronger foot against a back five; one-on-one dribbler likely to find space late as DR Congo's block fatigues.

assists.note: Primary creator from the No.10 role; league's standout assist man (21 PL assists), main set-piece and through-ball supplier to Ronaldo.
Overlapping left-back delivering cutbacks/crosses against a compact block; one of Portugal's most advanced full-back outlets.
Deep-lying playmaker controlling tempo; line-breaking passes to release Neto/Bernardo and feed the box against a low block.

penalty.note: DR Congo defend deep with a packed box (Wan-Bissaka, Mbemba, Tuanzebe, Masuaku), raising the chance of a box foul over 90 minutes. Ronaldo is the established first-choice penalty taker, Bruno Fernandes the reliable backup.

lineups.home: Diogo Costa, Joao Cancelo, Tomas Araujo, Ruben Dias, Nuno Mendes, Joao Neves, Vitinha, Bernardo Silva, Bruno Fernandes, Joao Felix, Cristiano Ronaldo
lineups.away: Lionel Mpasi, Aaron Wan-Bissaka, Chancel Mbemba, Axel Tuanzebe, Arthur Masuaku, Meschack Elia, Samuel Moutoussamy, Noah Sadiki, Nathanael Mbuku, Cedric Bakambu, Yoane Wissa

playerNotes.note: 41, sixth World Cup, 226 caps / 143 goals (both records). First-choice penalty taker; led qualifying scoring with 5. The match's headline figure and main goal threat.
Back in his preferred No.10; record-breaking PL assist season. Primary creator, on free-kicks and backup penalties.
Defensive leader; had injury problems in the second half of the club season but is fit for the opener and expected to start at centre-back.
Star man and main attacking outlet; 9 international goals. Injury-hit, underwhelming Newcastle season, but DR Congo's chief counter-attacking threat.
Veteran striker, squad's top scorer (21 international goals); big-game experience, likely to feature as a focal point or impact option off the bench.
Premier League full-back anchoring a likely back five; key to containing Portugal's wide overloads from Neto and Nuno Mendes.

win.reason: England field their strongest XI with Kane in career-best form (61 goals for Bayern in 2025-26) and a deeper squad. Croatia leaving Kovacic on the bench weakens their midfield control against the Rice-Anderson pivot. England favored but Croatia's tournament experience and Modric keep it from being a rout.

scorers.note: England's penalty taker and the most prolific striker in the tournament (61 goals club 2025-26); central reference point and primary goal threat.
Driving from the No.10 role, arrives late in the box behind Kane; secondary goal threat from deep runs.
Starts on the left wing; pace in behind Croatia's back three, cutting inside onto his stronger foot.
Croatia's lone starting striker in red-hot form (12 in 13 MLS); plays his club football at this very AT&T Stadium for FC Dallas — main away threat fed by Baturina.

assists.note: Primary creator from the No.10, feeding Kane and the wingers.
Right-back and a corner/free-kick deliverer for England; overlaps Madueke and whips crosses for Kane.
Croatia's chief creative outlet in the advanced role; the designated supplier to Musa and a set-piece option.

penalty.backup: Bukayo Saka (England, from bench) / Ivan Perisic (Croatia)
penalty.note: Kane is England's automatic spot-kick taker. With Kovacic and Kramaric benched, Modric is Croatia's on-pitch penalty taker, with Perisic the next option and Budimir the bench backup. Modric, Perisic, Pasalic and Baturina share set-piece duties.

lineups.home: Jordan Pickford, Reece James, John Stones, Ezri Konsa, Nico O'Reilly, Elliot Anderson, Declan Rice, Noni Madueke, Jude Bellingham, Anthony Gordon, Harry Kane
lineups.away: Dominik Livakovic, Josip Sutalo, Luka Vuskovic, Josko Gvardiol, Josip Stanisic, Luka Sucic, Luka Modric, Ivan Perisic, Mario Pasalic, Martin Baturina, Petar Musa

playerNotes.note: Career-best form, 61 goals for Bayern in 2025-26 and second European Golden Shoe; penalty taker and focal point.
Starts at centre-back alongside Stones; Marc Guehi drops to the bench — a notable selection call.
Starts at left-back, the least-experienced England starter; Croatia may target his flank with Stanisic and Pasalic.
Captains at 40 after recovering from a cheekbone fracture; controls midfield and is the on-pitch penalty taker with Kovacic benched.
Preferred over Kramaric at striker; plays his MLS football at AT&T Stadium for FC Dallas, a familiarity edge in the venue.
BENCHED despite featuring in predicted XIs; his absence from the engine room weakens Croatia's ball progression against Rice and Anderson.

win.reason: Ghana lost 4 of their last 5 (conceding 11) and are without their entire spine - Kudus, Salisu AND Partey (denied Canada entry) all out. Panama are unbeaten through CONCACAF qualifying, defensively drilled under Christiansen, and Opta's supercomputer actually favors them (45%). Bookmakers shade Ghana on individual quality from Semenyo/Inaki Williams, but a depleted, leaky Ghana vs a compact, experienced Panama points to a tight, low-scoring stalemate in a tournament opener both sides must avoid losing.

scorers.note: Ghana's standout attacking threat with Kudus out; carries the forward line, in-form and direct from the left.
Captain, primary penalty taker (perfect 17/17 career record) and set-piece/free-kick option; 6 goals in qualifying Group I.
Panama's central striker, focal point of their counter-attacks and set-piece presence.
Pace in behind a high Panama line; off-bench or starting wide threat for Ghana.

assists.note: Chief creator/carrier with Kudus absent; cuts inside to feed runners.
Panama's creative engine, the link between defence and attack; main chance-creator and set-piece deliverer.
Wide creativity and crossing for Panama from the right/attacking flank.

penalty.note: Ayew is Ghana's designated penalty taker with a flawless 17/17 senior conversion record; for Panama, Carrasquilla/Murillo handle spot-kicks.

lineups.home: Ati-Zigi, Senaya, Adjetey, Opoku, Mensah, Owusu, Yirenkyi, Nuamah, Boakye, Semenyo, Ayew
lineups.away: Mosquera, Farina, Escobar, Cordoba, Murillo, Harvey, Godoy, Davis, Barcenas, Fajardo, Diaz

playerNotes.note: OUT - quadriceps injury with a rehab setback ended his season; ruled out of the World Cup entirely.
OUT for the opener - denied entry into Canada by host government; a huge loss to Ghana's midfield base.
OUT - injury; weakens Ghana's central defence, opening starts for Opoku/Adjetey.
Talisman with Kudus/Partey gone; Ghana's main goal threat and creator from the left.
Midfield orchestrator partnering captain Godoy; given freedom to dictate and create.
Russia 2018 veteran right wing-back; provides width, ball progression and defensive solidity.

win.reason: Colombia carry vastly superior European-based talent (Diaz at Bayern, Lucumi, Lerma, Sanchez) and warmed up with wins over Costa Rica and Jordan; Opta favours them at 62.4% and bookmakers price them heavy favourites. Uzbekistan are on their first-ever WC and lost warm-ups to Canada and Netherlands. Altitude at Azteca (2,250m) and a debut-tournament cagey opener are the only brakes on the margin.

scorers.note: Lead striker in the 4-2-3-1; main central finisher, in-form club season (the young Colombian Luis Javier Suarez, not the Uruguayan).
Colombia's most dangerous runner in behind, 15 Bundesliga goals; cuts in from left, also a penalty option.
Captain at No.10, primary set-piece + likely first-choice penalty taker.
Uzbekistan captain and all-time top scorer; lone outlet if they break, best chance on a set-piece/counter.

assists.note: Chief creator and dead-ball deliverer from the No.10 role.
Overlapping right-back, key crossing source down Colombia's right.
Pulls defenders and squares for the striker; cut-backs from the left.

penalty.note: Colombia likely to draw fouls vs a low block; Diaz/James share spot-kick duty, James the experienced backup. Altitude-tired defending late raises penalty odds slightly.

lineups.home: Yusupov; Ashurmatov, Khusanov, Urozov; Sayfiev, Mozgovoy, Shukurov, Nasrullaev; Fayzullaev, Masharipov; Shomurodov
lineups.away: Vargas; Munoz, Sanchez, Lucumi, Mojica; Lerma, Rios; Arias, Rodriguez, Diaz; Suarez

playerNotes.note: Man City centre-back, rising star; anchors the back three, key to containing Diaz/Suarez.
Doubt/fitness risk - missed both warm-ups with a back issue after a serious knee injury, no minutes in over a year.
Captain and all-time leading scorer; the lone genuine goal threat.
Bayern Munich winger, 15 Bundesliga goals; undisputed attacking leader making WC debut.
34yo captain at No.10; minor recent knock but declared fit; set-piece + penalty taker.
Did NOT make the 26-man squad - not available; striker duties fall to Luis Suarez.
